Note: 1700: Another sign of sloppiness. His TW is the same as his TA, except it looks like he added an extra ditto at 1700 in the Water column so the temperatures are offset by one hour from then on. In any case, it matters little because these are not really wet bulb temperatures, but it does illustrate this man's carelessness.
1100: His weather of rain and snow agrees with his dry bulb temp. His pressure drop at 0500 looks out to lunch. There is no wind and the pressure drops 0.74 inches in just three hours.
0000: It looks like his TW temperatures have become a little more realistic, though some are higher than the Dry Bulb temps.
2000: I adjusted his baro from 29.98 to 29.88. He has trouble coping.

People: 2200: At 10 P.M. the ceremony of marriage was performed between Lieut. Geo. W. De Long, U.S. Navy and Miss Emma Wolton of Le Havre by the Rev. Dr. Washington of the Episcopal Church of Le Havre.

Note: 0000: His first pressures were written as 30.02. The previous values were 30.52 so I have changed these four to 30.52.
